annotations.label: Shannon entropy of a discrete random variable (lead)

anchor.snippet: the entropy of a random variable quantifies the average level of uncertainty

annotations.note: Mathlib provides the per-summand function `Real.negMulLog` (x ↦ -x log x) used to build Shannon entropy, but no general `H(X) = ∑ -p_i log p_i` for an arbitrary discrete random variable.

annotations.label: Information content / self-information

anchor.snippet: The information content , also called the surprisal or self-information, of an event

annotations.note: No `selfInformation`/`surprisal` declaration was found in Mathlib (loogle returns 0 hits).

annotations.label: Entropy of a biased coin

anchor.snippet: Consider a coin with probability p of landing on heads

annotations.note: `Real.binEntropy p` is documented as the Shannon entropy of a Bernoulli random variable with success probability `p`.

annotations.label: Variable-length coding of A, B, C, D

anchor.snippet: consider the transmission of sequences comprising the 4 characters

annotations.note: No variable-length/Huffman-style coding example is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Shannon entropy Η of a discrete random variable

anchor.snippet: Shannon defined the entropy Η (Greek capital letter eta ) of a discrete random variable

annotations.note: Only the summand `Real.negMulLog` (x ↦ -x log x) is defined; the general sum `H(X) = -∑ p(x) log p(x)` over a discrete random variable is not packaged as a standalone declaration.

annotations.label: Convention 0 log_b 0 = 0

anchor.snippet: the value of the corresponding summand 0 log b (0) is taken to be 0

annotations.note: `Real.negMulLog_zero : Real.negMulLog 0 = 0` encodes the convention `0·log 0 = 0` used in entropy.

anchor.snippet: One may also define the conditional entropy of two variables

anchor.snippet: Let [MATH] be a sigma-algebra on

annotations.note: Mathlib has topological/dynamical entropy (e.g. `Mathlib.Dynamics.TopologicalEntropy.*`) but no entropy of a sigma-algebra on a probability space.

annotations.label: Coin toss entropy (Bernoulli process)

anchor.snippet: Consider tossing a coin with known, not necessarily fair, probabilities

annotations.note: `Real.binEntropy p = p · log p⁻¹ + (1-p) · log (1-p)⁻¹` is exactly the entropy of a (possibly biased) coin toss.

annotations.label: Shannon's four properties characterizing information

anchor.snippet: Shannon's solution of the fundamental properties of information

annotations.note: The axiomatic characterization of Shannon's information measure is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Shannon's characterization: I(p) = -k log p

anchor.snippet: Shannon discovered that a suitable choice of

annotations.note: Shannon's uniqueness theorem deriving I(p) = -k log p from the axioms is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Alternative axiomatic characterization (continuity, symmetry, maximum, increasing, additivity)

anchor.snippet: Another characterization of entropy uses the following properties

annotations.note: The Khinchin-style axiomatic characterization of entropy is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Additivity consequence: entropy of certain outcome is zero

anchor.snippet: The rule of additivity has the following consequences

annotations.note: Only specialised cases like `Real.binEntropy_zero`/`binEntropy_one` (entropy = 0 for a sure outcome) appear; the general axiomatic-additivity argument is not formalized.

annotations.note: The uniqueness statement from Aczél–Forte–Ng is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Further properties of Shannon entropy

anchor.snippet: The Shannon entropy satisfies the following properties

annotations.note: Concavity/nonnegativity/max-at-uniform-type properties are formalized only for `binEntropy`/`qaryEntropy` (e.g. `Real.strictConcave_binEntropy`, `Real.binEntropy_le_log_two`), not for general Shannon entropy.

annotations.label: Gibbs entropy

annotations.note: No Gibbs entropy declaration was found in Mathlib (loogle returns 0 hits for `Gibbs`).

annotations.label: von Neumann entropy

annotations.note: Mathlib has `VonNeumannAlgebra` machinery but no von Neumann entropy `-Tr(ρ log ρ)` of a density operator.

annotations.label: Relative entropy (Kullback–Leibler divergence)

anchor.snippet: It is defined as the Kullback–Leibler divergence from the distribution to a reference measure

annotations.note: `InformationTheory.klDiv μ ν` is the Kullback–Leibler divergence between two measures, defined via the log-likelihood ratio `MeasureTheory.llr`.

annotations.label: Efficiency / normalized entropy

anchor.snippet: This deficiency in entropy can be expressed as a ratio called efficiency

anchor.snippet: The proof follows as a simple corollary of Shearer's inequality

annotations.label: Entropy approximation to binomial coefficient

anchor.snippet: For integers 0 < k < n let q = k / n

annotations.note: No bound `n^{-1} log C(n,k) ≈ H(k/n)` was found (loogle finds no decl combining `choose`/`binomial` with `entropy`).
